title: Four Great Women And A Manicure

jokes.text: She had it all, except the most important thing. / Please don't say 'a man.' / No, a husband!
jokes.setup: Lisa anticipates the obvious sexist answer about needing a man
jokes.punchline: Marge delivers the exact same sentiment but with different wording
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons misdirection - sets up expectation of avoiding sexist trope, then delivers it anyway with semantic hair-splitting

jokes.text: The year was 1588, 400 years before Kirk Gibson would hit his famous home run
jokes.setup: Historical setting being established seriously
jokes.punchline: Completely random modern baseball reference as historical marker
jokes.explanation: Typical Simpsons historical non-sequitur - absurd but not particularly clever

jokes.text: So you can pump out a son, and then I don't gotta work for no broad no more... um... my liege
jokes.setup: Medieval advisor giving counsel to queen
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude modern misogyny breaks through the period setting
jokes.explanation: Homer's character bleeding through historical costume - solid character integration

jokes.text: Off with my head!
jokes.setup: Prince making entrance to woo queen
jokes.punchline: Ralph immediately suggests his own execution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph moment - his confusion creates instant comedy through character-specific stupidity

jokes.text: This is 1588, and his material is fresh and hip
jokes.setup: Queen defending her attraction to court jester
jokes.punchline: Using modern comedy industry language about 400-year-old material
jokes.explanation: Clever anachronism joke about comedy material freshness - good wordplay

jokes.text: If I ever have sex, this will be great afterwards
jokes.setup: Queen trying tobacco for first time
jokes.punchline: Admits her virginity while praising post-coital smoking
jokes.explanation: Good character moment combining historical fact (Elizabeth's virginity) with adult humor

jokes.text: Now take me to the real ones. / Those are the real ones
jokes.setup: King dismissing ships as inadequate, asking for real armada
jokes.punchline: Reveals the 'toy ships' are actually the mighty Spanish Armada
jokes.explanation: Classic visual gag setup with good payoff - plays on historical knowledge

jokes.text: Nice things? / No, not nice things!
jokes.setup: King orders torture with euphemistic 'put things inside of him'
jokes.punchline: Guard's innocent clarification met with sinister correction
jokes.explanation: Dark humor through innocent misunderstanding - economical dialogue

jokes.text: you have the strength of a plow horse, but the charm of a cart horse!
jokes.setup: Queen attempting romantic compliment
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment comparing lover to different types of work animals
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with horse comparisons - good structure but mild impact

jokes.text: Um, which one of us are you talking to?
jokes.setup: Queen declares love during jealous rage
jokes.punchline: Lover confused about who she's addressing in her declaration
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: Whoa, someone call Beowulf! Grendel got in again!
jokes.setup: Queen entering dungeon dramatically
jokes.punchline: Guard mistakes her for monster from Old English epic
jokes.explanation: Clever literary reference that works on multiple levels - good originality

jokes.text: Armada? What's armada? / Nothing. What's armada with you?
jokes.setup: Messenger accidentally mentions armada
jokes.punchline: Queen makes terrible pun with 'what's the matter'
jokes.explanation: Groan-worthy dad joke - intentionally bad pun humor

jokes.text: our fat, mean king could dump his faithful wife, we know you're on our side. So please destroy these horrible monsters who believe your mother should be revered
jokes.setup: Admiral praying before battle
jokes.punchline: Admits Henry VIII's selfish motives while demonizing Catholics for honoring Mary
jokes.explanation: Sharp historical satire exposing religious hypocrisy - very clever writing

jokes.text: So please destroy these horrible monsters who believe your mother should be revered
jokes.setup: English Protestant prayer asking for God's help
jokes.punchline: Describing Catholics as monsters for venerating Mary, Jesus's mother
jokes.explanation: Clever religious irony - pointing out the absurdity of calling people monsters for revering Jesus's mother

jokes.text: Normally, I love to see flaming dreamboats heading my way, but not like this, baby, not like this!
jokes.setup: Spanish ships on fire approaching
jokes.punchline: Sailor makes innuendo about 'flaming dreamboats' while ship burns
jokes.explanation: Good double entendre with perfect timing in crisis moment

jokes.text: Yeah, you keep telling yourself that
jokes.setup: Queen declares she doesn't need a man because she has England
jokes.punchline: Cynical response deflating the noble sentiment
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for Selma - cynical deflation of romantic ideals

jokes.text: The story you're about to tell is the copyrighted property of The Walt Disney Corporation
jokes.setup: Lisa beginning to tell Snow White story
jokes.punchline: Lawyer appearing to claim copyright on public domain fairy tale
jokes.explanation: Sharp corporate satire about Disney's aggressive copyright claims

jokes.text: There's Crabby... Drunky... Hungry! Greedy! Lenny! Kearney! and Doc... tor Hibbert
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to avoid Disney copyright with 'original' dwarfs
jokes.punchline: Names obviously Springfield characters, awkwardly adding 'tor' to Doc
jokes.explanation: Good callback humor with awkward copyright dodging

jokes.text: Ho-hi, ho-hi, it's off to work go I / This song's not like any song you know, ho-hi!
jokes.setup: Obvious Snow White work song parody
jokes.punchline: Self-aware lyrics claiming it's totally original
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about obvious copyright infringement

jokes.text: Also, there's a weather girl on channel nine who's... (rolls tongue, giggles)
jokes.setup: Magic mirror declaring Snow White fairest
jokes.punchline: Gets distracted by attractive weather reporter
jokes.explanation: Modern twist on classic fairy tale with mild sexual humor

jokes.text: I- I-I cannot do it! Run, lassie! Find wee little men, and hide with them!
jokes.setup: Huntsman can't kill Snow White
jokes.punchline: Willie's Scottish accent and phrasing in fairy tale context
jokes.explanation: Good character integration but fairly predictable Willie moment

jokes.text: Maybe I'll make her a heart out of construction paper. I- I-I cannot do it!
jokes.setup: Moe can't kill anything, tries construction paper alternative
jokes.punchline: Moe can't even do arts and crafts
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation joke - taking Moe's inability to extreme absurd lengths where he can't even do harmless crafts. Perfect economy and character work

jokes.text: I- I-I cannot do it! Maybe I'll make her a heart out of construction paper
jokes.setup: Willie can't kill pig for fake heart either
jokes.punchline: Suggests craft project as murder substitute
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation from murder to arts and crafts - good contrast

jokes.text: This bed's too hard. This bed's too soft. And this bed is just... like the first one, also too hard
jokes.setup: Classic Goldilocks bed-testing sequence
jokes.punchline: Third bed isn't 'just right' but same as first
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of fairy tale expectation - anticlimatic but clever

jokes.text: Ho-hi, ho-hi, it's time to now to get high / We'll take some shrooms and go to our rooms
jokes.setup: Dwarfs returning from work singing
jokes.punchline: Song about recreational drug use
jokes.explanation: Juvenile drug humor - fits the subversive fairy tale theme but not particularly clever

jokes.text: You're in marketing. Why do you even bring an axe? / If you were in marketing, you'd know
jokes.setup: Question about marketing dwarf having weapon
jokes.punchline: Implies marketing requires violent tools
jokes.explanation: Sharp corporate satire about marketing ruthlessness

jokes.text: (sighs) Guess it's you and me again, doorknob. (makes kissing sounds)
jokes.setup: Snow White rejects kiss from handsome dwarf
jokes.punchline: Dwarf resignedly kisses doorknob instead
jokes.explanation: Good pathetic character moment with visual payoff

jokes.text: (sarcastically): Ooh, herbivores. I'm so scared
jokes.setup: Forest animals attacking evil queen
jokes.punchline: Queen's sarcastic dismissal of vegetarian animals
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic line with biological humor - efficient delivery

jokes.text: she couldn't indulge in strenuous activities like handball, but otherwise she was fine
jokes.setup: Snow White brought back to life by lady doctor
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific athletic restriction
jokes.explanation: Absurdly specific medical restriction - good deflation of happy ending

jokes.text: All I have are some dry-cleaning coupons. / Deal. / Thanks, Marge. But don't forget, there's still gonna be a civil suit
jokes.setup: Homer needs bribe money
jokes.punchline: Police accept coupons as bribe, then mention additional lawsuit
jokes.explanation: Good corruption humor with escalating consequences

jokes.text: If you don't like getting barbecue sauce out of a leotard, why did you get into show business?
jokes.setup: Marge complaining about costume cleaning
jokes.punchline: Homer's bizarre understanding of show business
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - absurd but delivered with complete confidence

jokes.text: Mel's like the son Laurence Olivier and John Gielgud always wanted, but never had. But, oh, how they tried
jokes.setup: Praising Mel's acting ability
jokes.punchline: Implies the two legendary actors had a sexual relationship
jokes.explanation: Sophisticated innuendo with theatrical knowledge - very clever construction

jokes.text: I think Mel would be happier as an understudy. / A six-feet-understudy
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting Mel should have lesser role
jokes.punchline: Homer's death euphemism
jokes.explanation: Clever death euphemism with theater terminology - very economical

jokes.text: That 'crazy talk' is the work of the immortal Bard, William Shakespeare. / Well, if you see him in heaven, tell him he sucks
jokes.setup: Mel defending Shakespeare's language
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude dismissal of greatest writer in English
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer anti-intellectualism - character-specific philistinism

jokes.text: Today's Tuesday, so that would make it Friday, and then, uh...
jokes.setup: Homer attempting Shakespeare's 'tomorrow and tomorrow'
jokes.punchline: Completely fails at basic calendar math
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer stupidity - in character but not particularly innovative

jokes.text: (in girly voice): Conrad Birdie? Coming here to Sweet Apple? / That's Bye-Bye Birdie! / Damn it, Morpheus. Not everyone believes what you believe. / That's the screenplay for The Matrix Reloaded
jokes.setup: Homer confusing his scripts and roles
jokes.punchline: Series of increasingly random script mix-ups
jokes.explanation: Good confusion comedy but relies on pop culture references

jokes.text: Damn it, Morpheus. Not everyone believes what you believe. That's the screenplay for The Matrix Reloaded
jokes.setup: Homer continuing to quote wrong material
jokes.punchline: Quoting Matrix sequel in Shakespeare play
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture confusion but the Matrix reference feels somewhat forced and dated

jokes.text: Who the hell is Banquo? / He's the one getting the good reviews. / Which makes him the next one you've got to kill
jokes.setup: Homer confused about Banquo's importance
jokes.punchline: Marge immediately suggests murder based on reviews
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Lady Macbeth's ambition with modern context

jokes.text: Wouldn't it be easier if I just took acting lessons? / Screw your courage to the sticking-place and we'll not fail
jokes.setup: Homer suggests reasonable solution
jokes.punchline: Marge responds with Shakespeare quote encouraging murder
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between Homer's practicality and Lady Macbeth's ambition

jokes.text: What's that from? Bull Durham? Macbeth! Mac-who?
jokes.setup: Marge quoting Shakespeare to inspire Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer recognizes baseball movie but not the play he's in
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priorities - knows sports movies but not classic literature, even when he's performing it

jokes.text: He who laughs last laughs dead
jokes.setup: Homer planning to kill Dr. Hibbert
jokes.punchline: Mangling the saying 'he who laughs last laughs best' with death threat
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay corruption of common saying - economical and character-appropriate dark humor

jokes.text: Killing makes me hungry / Eating makes me thirsty / Drinking makes me sleepy
jokes.setup: Homer singing about his murderous spree
jokes.punchline: Chain of mundane consequences from killing
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific approach to murder - reduces it to basic needs

jokes.text: Why did he have to kill everyone in their costumes?
jokes.setup: Mass murder in theater production
jokes.punchline: Concern focused on costume damage rather than deaths
jokes.explanation: Dark humor through misplaced priorities - good absurdist logic

jokes.text: No, it was Homer! Your dark ambition guided his murderous hand. / No. I just encouraged him. / Encouraged by withholding sex!
jokes.setup: Ghosts blaming Marge for murders
jokes.punchline: Reveals her manipulation method
jokes.explanation: Good relationship humor with escalating revelation of manipulation

jokes.text: You call a sandwich maker an artist, it's like an invitation to steal! A guy's going nani-nooni bananas in there!
jokes.setup: Theater director discussing Subway franchise
jokes.punchline: Rant about sandwich artist pretensions with nonsense words
jokes.explanation: Good Krusty rant with invented language - character-specific rambling

jokes.text: Me having to read all those plays would be the real tragedy
jokes.setup: Marge listing all of Shakespeare's great roles for Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer considers reading the plays worse than murder
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer line - prioritizes laziness over morality with great economy

jokes.text: Mom, isn't that book the bible of right-wing losers? / Yeah, but the guy on the book jacket is one sexy slice of beefcake
jokes.setup: Lisa critiquing The Fountainhead's politics
jokes.punchline: Marge focused entirely on cover model's attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire with good character contrast - substance vs. superficiality

jokes.text: Yeah, but the guy on the book jacket is one sexy slice of beefcake
jokes.setup: Lisa pointing out The Fountainhead is right-wing bible
jokes.punchline: Marge's shallow attraction to Ayn Rand's photo overriding political concerns
jokes.explanation: Good Marge characterization - her physical attraction overriding intellectual considerations

jokes.text: dancers shouldn't kick too high, and buildings mustn't reach the sky
jokes.setup: Teacher enforcing conformity
jokes.punchline: Rhyming rules that suppress achievement
jokes.explanation: Good satire of mediocrity enforcement with memorable rhyme

jokes.text: Your rubble is still a little bit higher than the others. So, let's just... There. Welcome to the real world, baby
jokes.setup: Teacher continuing to level Maggie's achievement
jokes.punchline: Treats destruction of creativity as life lesson
jokes.explanation: Dark satire about crushing individuality presented as wisdom

jokes.text: If I were you, there'd be a lot of strangled babies
jokes.setup: Homer commenting on teaching difficulty
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of infanticide as solution
jokes.explanation: Typically dark Homer solution - in character but disturbing

jokes.text: You will be tried as a toddler
jokes.setup: Maggie facing trial for her creativity
jokes.punchline: Legal system adapted for defendant's age
jokes.explanation: Clever legal joke with perfect economy - adapts adult concept for baby

jokes.text: Ho-hi, ho-hi, it's time to say good-bye / If Disney sues, we'll claim fair use
jokes.setup: Closing song in Disney parody style
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging potential copyright lawsuit
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier copyright theme with self-aware humor

jokes.text: We'll get take-out and the make out / We're bi, we're bi, we're bi, we're bi
jokes.setup: Continuing parody closing song
jokes.punchline: Rhyme leads to sexual orientation declaration
jokes.explanation: Mild sexual humor driven by rhyme scheme - somewhat forced

jokes.text: We're bi, we're bi, we're bi, we're bi
jokes.setup: Dwarfs singing closing song
jokes.punchline: Declaring sexual orientation in Snow White parody song
jokes.explanation: Weak ending joke - sexual orientation humor feels forced and not particularly clever or relevant
